 {"id":"brenner_bot-5so.12.2.3","title":"Task: Implement experiment post (encode + send DELTA message)","description":"# Goal
Optionally collapse encode+send into one operator command.

---

# Proposed UX
```bash
./brenner.ts experiment post \
  --thread-id RS-... \
  --sender OperatorName \
  --to BlueLake,PurpleMountain,RedForest \
  --test-id T1 \
  --result-file results.json
```

This should:
- run the encoder
- send a DELTA message with subject `DELTA[results]: ...`

---

# Safety
- Posting is a side effect; require explicit command.

---

# Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `experiment post` sends a correct DELTA message to the thread
- [ ] Errors do not lose result files (fail loud)

 {"id":"brenner_bot-5so.12.2.4","title":"Task: E2E test: bind target repo → run experiment → compile updated artifact","description":"# Goal
Prove the full loop works end-to-end on a tiny fixture repo.

---

# Test flow
1) Create a temporary target repo fixture (or use a checked-in small fixture directory)
2) Start a session with `--target-repo`
3) Run an experiment command in sandbox
4) Encode results to DELTAs
5) Post DELTA to Agent Mail thread (or simulate by injecting message body)
6) Compile artifact and assert expected sections updated

---

# Acceptance criteria
- [ ] End-to-end test covers the full pipeline without mocks (where feasible)

 {"id":"brenner_bot-5so.12.2.5","title":"Task: Update runbook for experiment-backed sessions","description":"# Goal
Add a copy/paste operator workflow for experiment-backed sessions.

---

# Include
- How to bind target repo
- How to define a discriminative test command
- How to run it safely
- How to encode/post results
- How to compile/publish updated artifact

---

# Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Operators can run the loop without inventing commands
","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:05:12.580515238-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-30T19:05:12.580515238-05:00","labels":["docs","experiments","runbook"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-5so.12.2.5","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-5so.12.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:05:12.609701715-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
+{"id":"brenner_bot-5so.12.3","title":"Feature: Web lab experiment panel (run + view + post results)","description":"# Goal
Make experiment-backed sessions usable from the web lab UI, not just the CLI.

---

# User experience
Within a session page (`/sessions/[threadId]`):
- operator can see the target repo binding
- operator can run a pre-declared test command (or paste one)
- operator can see stdout/stderr + exit code
- operator can convert the result into DELTAs and post them into the thread

---

# Security constraints
- Lab mode only (fail closed)
- Same auth requirements as other orchestration actions
- No network by default for runs

---

# Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Web UI can trigger an experiment run (via server-side API)
- [ ] Results are viewable and auditable
- [ ] Posting results creates DELTA messages in the thread

+{"id":"brenner_bot-5so.12.3.1","title":"Task: Add API route to run sandboxed experiments (lab mode)","description":"# Goal
Add a server-side API endpoint that triggers an experiment run using the same underlying runner as the CLI.

---

# Requirements
- Accept thread id + test command (or test id)
- Use SessionBinding to locate target repo
- Execute via sandbox runner
- Return structured result payload

---

# Security
- Require lab mode + orchestration auth
- No network by default

---

# Acceptance criteria
- [ ] API returns stdout/stderr/exit code/duration
- [ ] API fails closed when sandbox backend unavailable

+{"id":"brenner_bot-5so.12.3.2","title":"Task: Session page experiment panel UI (results viewer + post)","description":"# Goal
Add an experiment panel to the session page that:
- displays binding metadata
- runs experiments
- shows results
- posts encoded DELTAs back into the thread

---

# UX notes
- Make provenance obvious (command, timestamp).
- Encourage discriminative framing: link results back to T# and H#.

---

# Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Experiment panel visible in lab mode
- [ ] Results viewer shows stdout/stderr safely
- [ ] "Post DELTAs" creates a DELTA message in thread

+{"id":"brenner_bot-5so.12.3.3","title":"Task: E2E test: web experiment panel happy path","description":"# Goal
Cover the web experiment panel user journey.

---

# Test flow
- Start in lab mode (authenticated)
- Navigate to an existing session with binding
- Run a harmless command (fixture)
- Verify results render
- Post DELTAs
- Verify thread shows new DELTA message

---

# Acceptance criteria
- [ ] E2E test is deterministic (uses fixture)

+{"id":"brenner_bot-5so.13","title":"EPIC: Topic evidence packs v0 (papers/notes/datasets with stable anchors)","description":"# Goal
Enable BrennerBot sessions to operate on **arbitrary research subjects** (not just codebases) by supporting a lightweight "topic evidence pack" with stable anchors and citations.

---

# Why this matters
Right now, artifact provenance conventions are Brenner-transcript-specific (`§n`).
To do real research on a chosen subject, we need a way to:
- collect the minimal evidence we are using (papers, notes, dataset descriptions)
- cite it in artifacts with stable anchors
- avoid drifting into model-memory speculation

---

# Design stance (v0)
- Keep it *excerpt-first*: store only the evidence excerpts we actually use.
- Keep it *anchored*: every excerpt gets a stable ID (e.g., `S1.P3`, `S2#p5`).
- Keep it *local-first*: topic packs are for lab mode and local workflows, not public site.

---

# Non-goals (v0)
- A full bibliographic manager
- Crawling the web automatically
- Shipping copyrighted PDFs to production

---

# Definition of done
- A session can optionally attach a topic evidence pack.
- Artifacts can cite evidence pack anchors in addition to Brenner `§n` anchors.
- Search/browsing of evidence pack is possible in lab mode.

 {"id":"brenner_bot-5so.2","title":"EPIC: Corpus Engine (parse/search/quote-bank/excerpts)","description":"# Goal
Make the corpus usable as a *tool*, not just a document:
- fast full-text search across the transcript + distillations
- stable anchors (`§n`) and excerpt composition
- a quote bank tagged by operators/primitives

# Why this matters
The corpus is the "ground truth". If it’s hard to search, cite, and excerpt, the whole system drifts into ungrounded synthesis.

 {"id":"brenner_bot-5so.2.1","title":"Corpus: transcript section parser + structured index","description":"# Goal
Turn `complete_brenner_transcript.md` into a structured representation (sections keyed by `§n`) usable by:
- search
- excerpt composition
- citation links

# Acceptance criteria
- A parser can produce `{sectionId, title, content}` entries for all sections.
- Output is stable and deterministic.
